31	
32	import torch
33	import torch.nn.functional as F
34	from datasets import Dataset as HFDataset
35	from tqdm import tqdm
36	from transformers import PreTrainedTokenizer
37	
38	from ..distributed import get_draft_sp_group, get_sp_ring_group
39	from .parse import GeneralParser, GLMParser, HarmonyParser, ThinkingParser
40	from .template import TEMPLATE_REGISTRY, ChatTemplate
41	
42	# define a type called conversation
43	Conversation = List[Dict[str, str]]
44	
45	
46	# ==============================
47	# This file is for preprocessing the data
48	# ==============================
49	
50	
51	def _apply_loss_mask_from_chat_template(
52	    text: str,
53	    offsets: torch.Tensor,
54	    chat_template: ChatTemplate,
55	) -> torch.Tensor:
56	    """
57	    Apply loss mask to identify assistant response spans using chat template.
58	
59	    Args:
60	        text: The formatted conversation text.
61	        offsets: Token offset mapping from tokenizer.
62	        chat_template: The chat template to use for identifying assistant spans.
63	
64	    Returns:
65	        A tensor indicating which tokens should contribute to the loss (1) or not (0).
66	    """
67	    loss_mask = torch.zeros(len(offsets), dtype=torch.long)
68	
69	    user_message_separator = (
70	        f"{chat_template.end_of_turn_token}{chat_template.user_header}"
71	    )
72	    assistant_message_separator = (
73	        f"{chat_template.end_of_turn_token}{chat_template.assistant_header}"
74	    )
75	
76	    # Find spans of assistant responses using regex
77	    assistant_pattern = (
78	        re.escape(assistant_message_separator)
79	        + r"(.*?)(?="
80	        + re.escape(user_message_separator)
81	        + "|$)"
82	    )
83	
84	    matches_found = 0
85	
86	    for match in re.finditer(assistant_pattern, text, re.DOTALL):
87	        matches_found += 1
88	        # Assistant response text span (excluding assistant_header itself)
89	        assistant_start_char = match.start(1)
90	        assistant_end_char = match.end(1)
91	
92	        # Mark tokens overlapping with assistant response
93	        for idx, (token_start, token_end) in enumerate(offsets):
94	            # Token is part of the assistant response span
95	            if token_end <= assistant_start_char:
96	                continue  # token before assistant text
97	            if token_start > assistant_end_char:
98	                continue  # token after assistant text
99	            loss_mask[idx] = 1
100	
101	    if matches_found == 0:
102	        print("WARNING: No assistant response spans found in the conversation text.")
103	
104	    return loss_mask
105	
106	
107	# Copied from https://github.com/SafeAILab/EAGLE/blob/main/eagle/traineagle3/cnets.py
108	def preprocess_conversations(
109	    tokenizer: PreTrainedTokenizer,
110	    conversations: Union[List[Conversation], List[str]],
111	    chat_template: ChatTemplate,
112	    max_length: int = 2048,
113	    is_preformatted: bool = False,
114	    train_only_last_turn: bool = False,
115	    tools: Optional[List[List[Dict]]] = [[]],
116	    **kwargs,
117	) -> Dict[str, List[torch.Tensor]]:
118	    """
119	    Preprocess a batch of ShareGPT style conversations or pre-formatted text.
120	
121	    Args:
122	        tokenizer: The tokenizer to use for tokenization.
123	        conversations: A list of conversations (if is_preformatted=False) or
124	                      a list of pre-formatted text strings (if is_preformatted=True).
125	        chat_template: The chat template to use for formatting/identifying spans.
126	        max_length: The maximum length of the tokenized input.
127	        is_preformatted: Whether the input is already formatted text strings.
128	        train_only_last_turn: If True, only the last assistant turn contributes to the loss.
129	        tools: Optional list of tools information corresponding to each conversation, used for tool-use conversations.
130	
131	    Returns:
132	        A dictionary containing:
133	            - input_ids: List of tokenized input IDs.
134	            - loss_mask: List of loss masks indicating which tokens should contribute to the loss.
135	            - attention_mask: List of attention masks.
136	    """
137	    # prepare result
138	    results = {"input_ids": [], "loss_mask": [], "attention_mask": []}
139	    if chat_template.parser_type == "general":
140	        parser = GeneralParser(tokenizer, chat_template)
141	    elif chat_template.parser_type == "thinking":
142	        parser = ThinkingParser(tokenizer, chat_template)
143	    elif chat_template.parser_type == "glm":
144	        parser = GLMParser(tokenizer, chat_template)
145	    elif chat_template.parser_type == "openai-harmony":
146	        parser = HarmonyParser(tokenizer, chat_template)
147	    else:
148	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid parser type: {chat_template.parser_type}")
149	    kwargs_list = [{} for _ in range(len(conversations))]
150	    for key, value_list in kwargs.items():
151	        for i, value in enumerate(value_list):
152	            kwargs_list[i][key] = value
153	    for source, tool, kwargs_item in zip(conversations, tools, kwargs_list):
154	        if not source:
155	            # if the source is None, skip it
156	            continue
157	        input_ids, loss_mask = parser.parse(
158	            source,
159	            max_length,
160	            preformatted=is_preformatted,
161	            train_only_last_turn=train_only_last_turn,
162	            tool=tool,
163	            **kwargs_item,
164	        )
165	        results["input_ids"].append(input_ids[None, :])
166	        results["loss_mask"].append(loss_mask[None, :])
167	        results["attention_mask"].append(torch.ones_like(loss_mask)[None, :])
168	    return results
169	
170	
171	def build_eagle3_dataset(
172	    dataset: HFDataset,
173	    tokenizer: PreTrainedTokenizer,
174	    chat_template: Optional[str] = None,
175	    max_length: Optional[int] = 2048,
176	    shuffle_seed: Optional[int] = 42,
177	    num_proc: Optional[int] = 8,
178	    cache_dir: Optional[str] = None,
179	    cache_key: Optional[str] = None,
180	    is_preformatted: Optional[bool] = False,
181	    train_only_last_turn: Optional[bool] = False,
182	    minimum_valid_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
183	) -> HFDataset:
184	    """
185	    build eagle3 dataset
186	
187	    Args:
188	        dataset: HF dataset to process.
189	        tokenizer: The tokenizer to use for tokenization.
190	        chat_template: The chat template to use for formatting conversations.
191	                        This includes the system prompt and user/assistant tokens
192	                        required to delineate different parts of the conversation
193	                        for loss mask generation.
194	        max_length: The maximum length of the tokenized input.
195	        shuffle_seed: The seed for shuffling the dataset.
196	        num_proc: The number of processes to use for multiprocessing.
197	        cache_dir: The directory to use for caching the processed dataset.
198	        cache_key: The key to use for caching the processed dataset.
199	        is_preformatted: Whether the dataset contains preformatted text of the conversation
200	                        (e.g. includes system prompt, user and assistant start and end tokens)
201	                        and doesn't need to have the chat template applied.
202	                        Note that the chat_template still needs to be specified to determine
203	                        the assistant spans for loss mask generation.
204	                        If True, expects "text" column with ready-to-train text.
205	                        If False, expects "conversations" column with ShareGPT format.
206	        train_only_last_turn: If True, only the last assistant turn contributes to the loss.
207	                             Useful for thinking models where history may not contain thoughts.
208	        minimum_valid_tokens: If set, drops samples with fewer trainable tokens.
209	
210	    Returns:
211	        The processed HF dataset.
212	    """
213	    if minimum_valid_tokens is not None and minimum_valid_tokens < 0:
214	        raise ValueError("minimum_valid_tokens must be >= 0")
215	
216	    # Validate chat_template requirement
217	    if chat_template is None:
218	        raise ValueError("chat_template must be provided for all dataset types")
219	
220	    assert (
221	        chat_template in TEMPLATE_REGISTRY.get_all_template_names()
222	    ), f"Chat template {chat_template} not found in TEMPLATE_REGISTRY, you may need to register it first"
223	
224	    template: ChatTemplate = TEMPLATE_REGISTRY.get(chat_template)
225	
226	    dataset = dataset.shuffle(seed=shuffle_seed)
227	    original_cols = dataset.column_names
228	
229	    def preprocess_function(examples):
230	        # Handle different dataset formats
231	        if is_preformatted:
232	            # Handle pre-formatted text (should be in "text" column)
233	            if "text" not in examples:
234	                raise ValueError(
235	                    f"Expected 'text' column for is_preformatted=True, but found columns: {list(examples.keys())}"
236	                )
237	            processed = preprocess_conversations(
238	                tokenizer,
239	                examples["text"],
240	                template,
241	                max_length,
242	                is_preformatted=True,
243	                train_only_last_turn=train_only_last_turn,
244	            )
245	        else:
246	            # Handle ShareGPT conversations
247	            if "conversations" not in examples:
248	                raise ValueError(
249	                    f"Expected 'conversations' column for is_preformatted=False, but found columns: {list(examples.keys())}"
250	                )
251	            conversations = examples.pop("conversations")
252	            if "id" in examples:
253	                examples.pop("id")
254	            if "tools" in examples:
255	                tools_raw = examples.pop("tools")
256	                # Parse tools: handle JSON strings from safe_conversations_generator
257	                tools = []
258	                for tool_item in tools_raw:
259	                    if isinstance(tool_item, (str, list)):
260	                        try:
261	                            tools.append(json.loads(tool_item))
262	                        except json.JSONDecodeError:
263	                            warnings.warn(
264	                                f"Failed to parse tools JSON string: {tool_item[:100]}..."
265	                            )
266	                            tools.append([])
267	                    elif isinstance(tool_item, list):
268	                        tools.append(tool_item)
269	                    elif tool_item is None:
270	                        tools.append([])
271	                    else:
272	                        warnings.warn(
273	                            f"Unexpected tools type: {type(tool_item)}, using empty list"
274	                        )
275	                        tools.append([])
276	            else:
277	                tools = [[] for _ in range(len(conversations))]
278	            processed = preprocess_conversations(
279	                tokenizer,
280	                conversations,
281	                template,
282	                max_length,
283	                is_preformatted=False,
284	                train_only_last_turn=train_only_last_turn,
285	                tools=tools,
286	                **examples,
287	            )
288	
289	        return processed
290	
291	    # Process dataset only once
292	    if cache_dir and cache_key:
293	        load_from_cache_file = True
294	        os.makedirs(cache_dir, exist_ok=True)
295	        cache_file_name = os.path.join(cache_dir, f"{cache_key}.pkl")
296	        print(f"dataset is cached at {cache_file_name}")
297	    elif cache_dir is None and cache_key is None:
298	        load_from_cache_file = False
299	        cache_file_name = None
300	        print(f"dataset is not cached")
301	    else:
302	        warnings.warn(
303	            f"cache_dir and cache_key must be provided together to make caching work"
304	        )
305	
306	    # Disable tokenizers internal parallelism when using multiprocessing to avoid
307	    # deadlocks caused by forked Rust threads (see huggingface/tokenizers#1391).
308	    if num_proc is not None and num_proc > 1:
309	        os.environ["TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM"] = "false"
310	
311	    dataset = dataset.map(
312	        preprocess_function,
313	        batched=True,
314	        num_proc=num_proc,
315	        batch_size=1000,
316	        remove_columns=original_cols,
317	        # keep_in_memory=True,
318	        load_from_cache_file=load_from_cache_file,
319	        cache_file_name=cache_file_name,
320	    )
321	
322	    if minimum_valid_tokens is not None:
323	        before_filter = len(dataset)
324	
325	        def has_minimum_valid_tokens(example):
326	            loss_mask = example["loss_mask"]
327	            if isinstance(loss_mask, torch.Tensor):
328	                valid_tokens = int(loss_mask.sum().item())
329	            else:
330	                valid_tokens = sum(
331	                    int(token)
332	                    for row in loss_mask
333	                    for token in (row if isinstance(row, list) else [row])
334	                )
335	            return valid_tokens >= minimum_valid_tokens
336	
337	        dataset = dataset.filter(
338	            has_minimum_valid_tokens,
339	            num_proc=num_proc,
340	            desc=f"Filtering samples with >= {minimum_valid_tokens} trainable tokens",
341	        )
342	        print(
343	            f"Filtered dataset by trainable tokens: {before_filter} -> {len(dataset)}"
344	        )
345	
346	    dataset.set_format(type="torch")
347	    return dataset
348	
349	
350	# ==============================
351	# Offline Eagle3 Dataset
352	# ==============================
353	# modified from https://github.com/NickL77/BaldEagle/blob/master/train/modules/data/data.py
354	def list_local_files(path, suffixes=None):
355	    if suffixes is None:
356	        suffixes = [".ckpt", ".ckpt.gz"]
357	    datapaths = []
358	    for root, directories, files in os.walk(path):
359	        for file in files:
360	            file_path = os.path.join(root, file)
361	            datapaths.append(file_path)
362	    if suffixes:
363	        datapaths = [
364	            f_name
365	            for f_name in datapaths
366	            if any(f_name.endswith(suffix) for suffix in suffixes)
367	        ]
368	    datapaths.sort()  # Sort to ensure deterministic order across ranks
369	    return datapaths
370	
371	
372	class OfflineEagle3Dataset(torch.utils.data.Dataset):
373	    def __init__(
374	        self,
375	        datapath,
376	        transform=None,
377	        max_len=2048,
378	        ttt_length=1,
379	        use_usp_preprocess=False,
380	    ):
381	        """
382	        Args:
383	            datapath: List of file paths.
384	            transform: Optional transform to apply.
385	            max_len: Maximum sequence length to load.
386	            ttt_length: TTT overlap length used in USP preprocessing.
387	            use_usp_preprocess: Whether to shard all sequences with USP overlap in preprocessing.
388	        """
389	        self.datapaths = datapath
390	        self.transform = transform
391	        self._epoch = 0
392	        self.max_len = max_len
393	        self.ttt_length = ttt_length
394	        self.use_usp_preprocess = use_usp_preprocess
395	        if use_usp_preprocess:
396	            sp_group = get_draft_sp_group()
397	            self.sp_rank = torch.distributed.get_rank(sp_group)
398	            self.sp_size = torch.distributed.get_world_size(sp_group)
399	            ring_group = get_sp_ring_group()
400	            self.ring_rank = torch.distributed.get_rank(ring_group)
401	            self.sp_ring_size = torch.distributed.get_world_size(ring_group)
402	
403	    @staticmethod
404	    def process_data(data, max_len, transform=None):
405	        new_data = {}
406	        # Squeeze due to our data generation script adding a batch dimension
407	        hidden_state = data["aux_hidden_state"].squeeze(0)[:max_len][None, :]
408	        target = data["hidden_state"].squeeze(0)[:max_len][None, :]
409	
410	        input_ids = data["input_ids"][:max_len][None, :]
411	        loss_mask = data["loss_mask"][:max_len][None, :]
412	        loss_mask[0, -1] = 0
413	
414	        new_data["attention_mask"] = torch.ones_like(loss_mask, dtype=torch.long)
415	        new_data["loss_mask"] = loss_mask
416	        new_data["target"] = target
417	        new_data["hidden_state"] = hidden_state
418	        new_data["input_ids"] = input_ids
419	        if transform:
420	            new_data = transform(new_data)
421	        return new_data
422	
423	    @staticmethod
424	    def process_data_usp(
425	        data,
426	        max_len,
427	        ttt_length=1,
428	        transform=None,
429	        sp_rank=0,
430	        sp_size=1,
431	        ring_rank=0,
432	        sp_ring_size=1,
433	    ):
434	        """
435	        USP preprocess: shard all sequences by sp_rank and add TTT overlap.
436	        Each local sequence length = ceil(max_len / sp_size) + ttt_length.
437	        """
438	        new_data = {}
439	
440	        input_ids = data["input_ids"]
441	        if input_ids.ndim == 1:
442	            input_ids = input_ids.unsqueeze(0)
443	
444	        global_len = min(max_len, input_ids.shape[1])
445	        chunk_size = (global_len + sp_size - 1) // sp_size
446	        start = sp_rank * chunk_size
447	        local_len = chunk_size + ttt_length
448	
449	        end = min(start + local_len, global_len)
450	
451	        def _slice_and_pad(tensor):
452	            if tensor.ndim == 1:
453	                tensor = tensor.unsqueeze(0)
454	            tensor = tensor[:, :global_len]
455	            sliced = tensor[:, start : min(end, tensor.shape[1])]
456	            valid_len = sliced.shape[1]
457	            if valid_len < local_len:
458	                pad_len = local_len - valid_len
459	                if tensor.ndim == 2:
460	                    sliced = F.pad(sliced, (0, pad_len))
461	                else:
462	                    sliced = F.pad(sliced, (0, 0, 0, pad_len))
463	            return sliced.contiguous(), valid_len
464	
465	        if "aux_hidden_state" not in data or data["aux_hidden_state"] is None:
466	            raise KeyError("aux_hidden_state is required for OfflineEagle3Dataset")
467	        new_data["hidden_state"], _ = _slice_and_pad(data["aux_hidden_state"])
468	        new_data["target"], _ = _slice_and_pad(data["hidden_state"])
469	
470	        new_data["input_ids"], valid_len = _slice_and_pad(input_ids)
471	
472	        full_loss_mask = data["loss_mask"]
473	        if full_loss_mask.ndim == 1:
474	            full_loss_mask = full_loss_mask.unsqueeze(0)
475	
476	        full_loss_mask = full_loss_mask[:, :global_len].clone()
477	        if full_loss_mask.numel() > 0:
478	            full_loss_mask[0, -1] = 0
479	        new_data["loss_mask"], _ = _slice_and_pad(full_loss_mask)
480	
481	        local_len = new_data["input_ids"].shape[1]
482	        attention_mask = torch.zeros((1, local_len), dtype=torch.long)
483	        attention_mask[:, :valid_len] = 1
484	        new_data["attention_mask"] = attention_mask
485	
486	        # Position ids should align with Ulysses all2all-expanded sequence length.
487	        # Within each ring group there are sp_ulysses_size Ulysses peers; each holds a
488	        # distinct usp_chunk_size slice, so position IDs must differ by ulysses_rank offset.
489	        sp_ulysses_size = max(1, sp_size // sp_ring_size)
490	        usp_chunk_size = max(local_len - ttt_length, 0)
491	        ring_chunk = usp_chunk_size * sp_ulysses_size
492	        ulysses_rank = sp_rank % sp_ulysses_size
493	        ring_start = ring_rank * ring_chunk + ulysses_rank * usp_chunk_size
494	        new_data["position_ids"] = torch.arange(
495	            ring_start, ring_start + usp_chunk_size, dtype=torch.long
496	        ).unsqueeze(0)
497	
498	        if transform:
499	            new_data = transform(new_data)
500	
501	        return new_data
502	
503	    def __len__(self):
504	        return len(self.datapaths)
505	
506	    def _open_file(self, index):
507	        """
508	        Opens the file with memory mapping.
509	        This operation is virtually instant and consumes negligible RAM
510	        because no data is actually read from disk yet.
511	        """
512	        data_path = self.datapaths[index]
513	        if data_path.endswith(".gz"):
514	            with gzip.open(data_path, "rb") as f:
515	                return torch.load(io.BytesIO(f.read()), weights_only=False)
516	        return torch.load(data_path, weights_only=False, mmap=True)
517	
518	    def __getitem__(self, index):
519	        try:
520	            data = self._open_file(index)
521	        except Exception as e:
522	            print(f"ERROR Failed to load {self.datapaths[index]} with error {e}")
523	            data = self._open_file(0)
524	
525	        # 2. Read only specific bytes from disk
526	        if self.use_usp_preprocess:
527	            return self.process_data_usp(
528	                data,
529	                self.max_len,
530	                ttt_length=self.ttt_length,
531	                transform=self.transform,
532	                sp_rank=self.sp_rank,
533	                sp_size=self.sp_size,
534	                ring_rank=self.ring_rank,
535	                sp_ring_size=self.sp_ring_size,
536	            )
537	        return self.process_data(
538	            data,
539	            self.max_len,
540	            self.transform,
541	        )
542	
543	    def set_epoch(self, epoch):
544	        self._epoch = epoch
545	
546	
547	def build_offline_eagle3_dataset(
548	    hidden_states_path: str,
549	    max_len: int = 2048,
550	    ttt_length: int = 1,
551	    use_usp_preprocess: bool = False,
552	) -> torch.utils.data.Dataset:
553	
554	    return OfflineEagle3Dataset(
555	        list_local_files(hidden_states_path),
556	        max_len=max_len,
557	        ttt_length=ttt_length,
558	        use_usp_preprocess=use_usp_preprocess,
559	    )
560	
561	
562	# ==============================
563	# Vocab Mapping
564	# ==============================
565	def generate_vocab_mapping_file(
566	    dataset: HFDataset,
567	    target_vocab_size: int,
568	    draft_vocab_size: int,
569	    cache_dir: str = "./cache/vocab_mapping",
570	    cache_key: str = "vocab_mapping",
571	) -> str:
572	    """
573	    Generate a vocab mapping file for the dataset.
574	
575	    Args:
576	        dataset: The dataset to process.
577	        target_vocab_size: The target vocabulary size.
578	        draft_vocab_size: The draft vocabulary size.
579	        cache_dir: The directory to use for caching the vocab mapping file.
580	        cache_key: The key to use for caching the vocab mapping file.
581	
582	    Returns:
583	        The path to the vocab mapping file.
584	    """
585	    # prepare cache directory
586	    os.makedirs(cache_dir, exist_ok=True)
587	    vocab_mapping_path = os.path.join(cache_dir, f"{cache_key}.pt")
588	
589	    if os.path.exists(vocab_mapping_path):
590	        print(f"Loading vocab mapping from the cached file at: {vocab_mapping_path}")
591	        return vocab_mapping_path
592	
593	    # we first count the frequency of effective tokens in the dataset
594	    token_dict = Counter()
595	    for input_ids, loss_mask in tqdm(
596	        zip(dataset["input_ids"], dataset["loss_mask"]),
597	        total=len(dataset),
598	        desc="Counting tokens for vocab mapping",
599	    ):
600	        masked_ids = input_ids[loss_mask == 1]
601	        unique_ids, counts = masked_ids.unique(return_counts=True)
602	        batch_token_dict = dict(zip(unique_ids.tolist(), counts.tolist()))
603	        token_dict.update(batch_token_dict)
604	
605	    # generate the d2t and t2d mapping
606	    d2t, t2d = process_token_dict_to_mappings(
607	        token_dict,
608	        draft_vocab_size,
609	        target_vocab_size,
610	    )
611	
612	    vocab_mapping = {
613	        "d2t": d2t,
614	        "t2d": t2d,
615	    }
616	    torch.save(vocab_mapping, vocab_mapping_path)
617	    print(f"Saved vocab mapping to: {vocab_mapping_path}")
618	    return vocab_mapping_path
619	
620	
621	def process_offline_eagle3_sample(
622	    raw: Dict[str, torch.Tensor], max_len: int
623	) -> Dict[str, torch.Tensor]:
624	    """Normalize one prepared EAGLE3 feature sample for the canonical loader.
625	
626	    Hidden-state preparation stores the target final state under
627	    hidden_state and auxiliary layers under aux_hidden_state. Training consumes
628	    those tensors as target and hidden_state respectively.
629	    """
630	    hidden_state = raw["aux_hidden_state"].squeeze(0)[:max_len].unsqueeze(0)
631	    target = raw["hidden_state"].squeeze(0)[:max_len].unsqueeze(0)
632	    input_ids = raw["input_ids"][:max_len].unsqueeze(0)
633	    loss_mask = raw["loss_mask"][:max_len].clone().unsqueeze(0)
634	    if loss_mask.numel() > 0:
635	        loss_mask[0, -1] = 0
636	
637	    return {
638	        "attention_mask": torch.ones_like(loss_mask, dtype=torch.long),
639	        "loss_mask": loss_mask,
640	        "target": target,
641	        "hidden_state": hidden_state,
642	        "input_ids": input_ids,
643	    }
644	
645	
646	def process_offline_dflash_sample(
647	    raw: Dict[str, torch.Tensor], max_len: int
648	) -> Dict[str, torch.Tensor]:
649	    """Normalize one prepared DFlash-family feature sample.
650	
651	    DFlash and Domino consume the same capture contract: token ids, a loss
652	    mask, and the concatenated target-layer states.  Unlike EAGLE3, there is no
653	    auxiliary/final-state swap and no target distribution.  Offline feature
654	    files may store ``hidden_states`` as either ``[seq, width]`` or
655	    ``[1, seq, width]``; the canonical loader always receives a leading batch
656	    dimension.
657	    """
658	    input_ids = raw["input_ids"][:max_len].unsqueeze(0)
659	    loss_mask = raw["loss_mask"][:max_len].unsqueeze(0)
660	    hidden_states = raw["hidden_states"]
661	    if hidden_states.dim() == 3:
662	        if hidden_states.shape[0] != 1:
663	            raise ValueError(
664	                "offline DFlash hidden_states must have shape [seq, width] or "
665	                f"[1, seq, width], got {tuple(hidden_states.shape)}"
666	            )
667	        hidden_states = hidden_states.squeeze(0)
668	    if hidden_states.dim() != 2:
669	        raise ValueError(
670	            "offline DFlash hidden_states must have shape [seq, width] or "
671	            f"[1, seq, width], got {tuple(hidden_states.shape)}"
672	        )
673	    hidden_states = hidden_states[:max_len].unsqueeze(0)
674	
675	    sequence_lengths = {
676	        input_ids.shape[1],
677	        loss_mask.shape[1],
678	        hidden_states.shape[1],
679	    }
680	    if len(sequence_lengths) != 1:
681	        raise ValueError(
682	            "offline DFlash features have mismatched sequence lengths after "
683	            f"truncation: input_ids={input_ids.shape[1]}, "
684	            f"loss_mask={loss_mask.shape[1]}, "
685	            f"hidden_states={hidden_states.shape[1]}"
686	        )
687	    return {
688	        "input_ids": input_ids,
689	        "loss_mask": loss_mask,
690	        "hidden_states": hidden_states,
691	    }
692	
693	
694	def process_token_dict_to_mappings(
695	    token_dict: Counter,
696	    draft_vocab_size: int,
697	    target_vocab_size: int,
698	) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
699	    """
700	    Process token_dict to create d2t and t2d mappings, with optional caching.
701	
702	    Args:
703	        token_dict: A Counter object mapping token ids to their frequencies.
704	        draft_vocab_size: The size of the draft vocabulary.
705	        target_vocab_size: The size of the target vocabulary.
706	
707	    Returns:
708	        A tuple containing:
709	            - d2t: A tensor mapping draft token ids to target token ids.
710	            - t2d: A tensor mapping target token ids to draft token ids.
711	    """
712	    if len(token_dict) < draft_vocab_size:
713	        existing_tokens = set(token_dict.keys())
714	        missing_tokens = set(range(draft_vocab_size)) - existing_tokens
715	        for token in missing_tokens:
716	            token_dict[token] = 0
717	            if len(token_dict) >= draft_vocab_size:
718	                break
719	    print(f"Added missing tokens to reach draft vocab size: {draft_vocab_size}")
720	    print(f"Total tokens after addition: {len(token_dict)}")
721	    total_frequency = sum(token_dict.values())
722	    top_N = token_dict.most_common(draft_vocab_size)
723	    top_N_frequency_sum = sum(freq for key, freq in top_N)
724	
725	    if total_frequency == 0:
726	        print(
727	            "Warning: Total token frequency is zero. All tokens will have zero ratio."
728	        )
729	        top_N_ratio = 0.0
730	    else:
731	        top_N_ratio = top_N_frequency_sum / total_frequency
732	
733	    print(f"top {draft_vocab_size} token frequency ratio: {top_N_ratio:.2%}")
734	    used_tokens = [key for key, freq in top_N]
735	    used_tokens.sort()
736	
737	    d2t = [used_tokens[i] - i for i in range(len(used_tokens))]
738	    t2d = [i in used_tokens for i in range(target_vocab_size)]
739	    d2t = torch.tensor(d2t)
740	    t2d = torch.tensor(t2d)
741	
742	    return d2t, t2d
743

# coding=utf-8
# Copyright 2022 EleutherAI and the HuggingFace Inc. team. All rights reserved.
#
# This code is based on EleutherAI's GPT-NeoX library and the GPT-NeoX
# and OPT implementations in HuggingFace Transformers.
# Portions of this code are adapted from:
#   - https://github.com/EleutherAI/gpt-neox (Apache License 2.0)
#   - https://github.com/huggingface/transformers (Apache License 2.0)
#   - https://github.com/SafeAILab/EAGLE (Apache License 2.0)
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.

import gzip
import io
import json
import os
import re
import warnings
from collections import Counter
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Tuple, Union

import torch
import torch.nn.functional as F
from datasets import Dataset as HFDataset
from tqdm import tqdm
from transformers import PreTrainedTokenizer

from ..distributed import get_draft_sp_group, get_sp_ring_group
from .parse import GeneralParser, GLMParser, HarmonyParser, ThinkingParser
from .template import TEMPLATE_REGISTRY, ChatTemplate

# define a type called conversation
Conversation = List[Dict[str, str]]


# ==============================
# This file is for preprocessing the data
# ==============================


def _apply_loss_mask_from_chat_template(
    text: str,
    offsets: torch.Tensor,
    chat_template: ChatTemplate,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Apply loss mask to identify assistant response spans using chat template.

    Args:
        text: The formatted conversation text.
        offsets: Token offset mapping from tokenizer.
        chat_template: The chat template to use for identifying assistant spans.

    Returns:
        A tensor indicating which tokens should contribute to the loss (1) or not (0).
    """
    loss_mask = torch.zeros(len(offsets), dtype=torch.long)

    user_message_separator = (
        f"{chat_template.end_of_turn_token}{chat_template.user_header}"
    )
    assistant_message_separator = (
        f"{chat_template.end_of_turn_token}{chat_template.assistant_header}"
    )

    # Find spans of assistant responses using regex
    assistant_pattern = (
        re.escape(assistant_message_separator)
        + r"(.*?)(?="
        + re.escape(user_message_separator)
        + "|$)"
    )

    matches_found = 0

    for match in re.finditer(assistant_pattern, text, re.DOTALL):
        matches_found += 1
        # Assistant response text span (excluding assistant_header itself)
        assistant_start_char = match.start(1)
        assistant_end_char = match.end(1)

        # Mark tokens overlapping with assistant response
        for idx, (token_start, token_end) in enumerate(offsets):
            # Token is part of the assistant response span
            if token_end <= assistant_start_char:
                continue  # token before assistant text
            if token_start > assistant_end_char:
                continue  # token after assistant text
            loss_mask[idx] = 1

    if matches_found == 0:
        print("WARNING: No assistant response spans found in the conversation text.")

    return loss_mask


# Copied from https://github.com/SafeAILab/EAGLE/blob/main/eagle/traineagle3/cnets.py
def preprocess_conversations(
    tokenizer: PreTrainedTokenizer,
    conversations: Union[List[Conversation], List[str]],
    chat_template: ChatTemplate,
    max_length: int = 2048,
    is_preformatted: bool = False,
    train_only_last_turn: bool = False,
    tools: Optional[List[List[Dict]]] = [[]],
    **kwargs,
) -> Dict[str, List[torch.Tensor]]:
    """
    Preprocess a batch of ShareGPT style conversations or pre-formatted text.

    Args:
        tokenizer: The tokenizer to use for tokenization.
        conversations: A list of conversations (if is_preformatted=False) or
                      a list of pre-formatted text strings (if is_preformatted=True).
        chat_template: The chat template to use for formatting/identifying spans.
        max_length: The maximum length of the tokenized input.
        is_preformatted: Whether the input is already formatted text strings.
        train_only_last_turn: If True, only the last assistant turn contributes to the loss.
        tools: Optional list of tools information corresponding to each conversation, used for tool-use conversations.

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing:
            - input_ids: List of tokenized input IDs.
            - loss_mask: List of loss masks indicating which tokens should contribute to the loss.
            - attention_mask: List of attention masks.
    """
    # prepare result
    results = {"input_ids": [], "loss_mask": [], "attention_mask": []}
    if chat_template.parser_type == "general":
        parser = GeneralParser(tokenizer, chat_template)
    elif chat_template.parser_type == "thinking":
        parser = ThinkingParser(tokenizer, chat_template)
    elif chat_template.parser_type == "glm":
        parser = GLMParser(tokenizer, chat_template)
    elif chat_template.parser_type == "openai-harmony":
        parser = HarmonyParser(tokenizer, chat_template)
    else:
        raise ValueError(f"Invalid parser type: {chat_template.parser_type}")
    kwargs_list = [{} for _ in range(len(conversations))]
    for key, value_list in kwargs.items():
        for i, value in enumerate(value_list):
            kwargs_list[i][key] = value
    for source, tool, kwargs_item in zip(conversations, tools, kwargs_list):
        if not source:
            # if the source is None, skip it
            continue
        input_ids, loss_mask = parser.parse(
            source,
            max_length,
            preformatted=is_preformatted,
            train_only_last_turn=train_only_last_turn,
            tool=tool,
            **kwargs_item,
        )
        results["input_ids"].append(input_ids[None, :])
        results["loss_mask"].append(loss_mask[None, :])
        results["attention_mask"].append(torch.ones_like(loss_mask)[None, :])
    return results


def build_eagle3_dataset(
    dataset: HFDataset,
    tokenizer: PreTrainedTokenizer,
    chat_template: Optional[str] = None,
    max_length: Optional[int] = 2048,
    shuffle_seed: Optional[int] = 42,
    num_proc: Optional[int] = 8,
    cache_dir: Optional[str] = None,
    cache_key: Optional[str] = None,
    is_preformatted: Optional[bool] = False,
    train_only_last_turn: Optional[bool] = False,
    minimum_valid_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
) -> HFDataset:
    """
    build eagle3 dataset

    Args:
        dataset: HF dataset to process.
        tokenizer: The tokenizer to use for tokenization.
        chat_template: The chat template to use for formatting conversations.
                        This includes the system prompt and user/assistant tokens
                        required to delineate different parts of the conversation
                        for loss mask generation.
        max_length: The maximum length of the tokenized input.
        shuffle_seed: The seed for shuffling the dataset.
        num_proc: The number of processes to use for multiprocessing.
        cache_dir: The directory to use for caching the processed dataset.
        cache_key: The key to use for caching the processed dataset.
        is_preformatted: Whether the dataset contains preformatted text of the conversation
                        (e.g. includes system prompt, user and assistant start and end tokens)
                        and doesn't need to have the chat template applied.
                        Note that the chat_template still needs to be specified to determine
                        the assistant spans for loss mask generation.
                        If True, expects "text" column with ready-to-train text.
                        If False, expects "conversations" column with ShareGPT format.
        train_only_last_turn: If True, only the last assistant turn contributes to the loss.
                             Useful for thinking models where history may not contain thoughts.
        minimum_valid_tokens: If set, drops samples with fewer trainable tokens.

    Returns:
        The processed HF dataset.
    """
    if minimum_valid_tokens is not None and minimum_valid_tokens < 0:
        raise ValueError("minimum_valid_tokens must be >= 0")

    # Validate chat_template requirement
    if chat_template is None:
        raise ValueError("chat_template must be provided for all dataset types")

    assert (
        chat_template in TEMPLATE_REGISTRY.get_all_template_names()
    ), f"Chat template {chat_template} not found in TEMPLATE_REGISTRY, you may need to register it first"

    template: ChatTemplate = TEMPLATE_REGISTRY.get(chat_template)

    dataset = dataset.shuffle(seed=shuffle_seed)
    original_cols = dataset.column_names

    def preprocess_function(examples):
        # Handle different dataset formats
        if is_preformatted:
            # Handle pre-formatted text (should be in "text" column)
            if "text" not in examples:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"Expected 'text' column for is_preformatted=True, but found columns: {list(examples.keys())}"
                )
            processed = preprocess_conversations(
                tokenizer,
                examples["text"],
                template,
                max_length,
                is_preformatted=True,
                train_only_last_turn=train_only_last_turn,
            )
        else:
            # Handle ShareGPT conversations
            if "conversations" not in examples:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"Expected 'conversations' column for is_preformatted=False, but found columns: {list(examples.keys())}"
                )
            conversations = examples.pop("conversations")
            if "id" in examples:
                examples.pop("id")
            if "tools" in examples:
                tools_raw = examples.pop("tools")
                # Parse tools: handle JSON strings from safe_conversations_generator
                tools = []
                for tool_item in tools_raw:
                    if isinstance(tool_item, (str, list)):
                        try:
                            tools.append(json.loads(tool_item))
                        except json.JSONDecodeError:
                            warnings.warn(
                                f"Failed to parse tools JSON string: {tool_item[:100]}..."
                            )
                            tools.append([])
                    elif isinstance(tool_item, list):
                        tools.append(tool_item)
                    elif tool_item is None:
                        tools.append([])
                    else:
                        warnings.warn(
                            f"Unexpected tools type: {type(tool_item)}, using empty list"
                        )
                        tools.append([])
            else:
                tools = [[] for _ in range(len(conversations))]
            processed = preprocess_conversations(
                tokenizer,
                conversations,
                template,
                max_length,
                is_preformatted=False,
                train_only_last_turn=train_only_last_turn,
                tools=tools,
                **examples,
            )

        return processed

    # Process dataset only once
    if cache_dir and cache_key:
        load_from_cache_file = True
        os.makedirs(cache_dir, exist_ok=True)
        cache_file_name = os.path.join(cache_dir, f"{cache_key}.pkl")
        print(f"dataset is cached at {cache_file_name}")
    elif cache_dir is None and cache_key is None:
        load_from_cache_file = False
        cache_file_name = None
        print(f"dataset is not cached")
    else:
        warnings.warn(
            f"cache_dir and cache_key must be provided together to make caching work"
        )

    # Disable tokenizers internal parallelism when using multiprocessing to avoid
    # deadlocks caused by forked Rust threads (see huggingface/tokenizers#1391).
    if num_proc is not None and num_proc > 1:
        os.environ["TOKENIZERS_PARALLELISM"] = "false"

    dataset = dataset.map(
        preprocess_function,
        batched=True,
        num_proc=num_proc,
        batch_size=1000,
        remove_columns=original_cols,
        # keep_in_memory=True,
        load_from_cache_file=load_from_cache_file,
        cache_file_name=cache_file_name,
    )

    if minimum_valid_tokens is not None:
        before_filter = len(dataset)

        def has_minimum_valid_tokens(example):
            loss_mask = example["loss_mask"]
            if isinstance(loss_mask, torch.Tensor):
                valid_tokens = int(loss_mask.sum().item())
            else:
                valid_tokens = sum(
                    int(token)
                    for row in loss_mask
                    for token in (row if isinstance(row, list) else [row])
                )
            return valid_tokens >= minimum_valid_tokens

        dataset = dataset.filter(
            has_minimum_valid_tokens,
            num_proc=num_proc,
            desc=f"Filtering samples with >= {minimum_valid_tokens} trainable tokens",
        )
        print(
            f"Filtered dataset by trainable tokens: {before_filter} -> {len(dataset)}"
        )

    dataset.set_format(type="torch")
    return dataset


# ==============================
# Offline Eagle3 Dataset
# ==============================
# modified from https://github.com/NickL77/BaldEagle/blob/master/train/modules/data/data.py
def list_local_files(path, suffixes=None):
    if suffixes is None:
        suffixes = [".ckpt", ".ckpt.gz"]
    datapaths = []
    for root, directories, files in os.walk(path):
        for file in files:
            file_path = os.path.join(root, file)
            datapaths.append(file_path)
    if suffixes:
        datapaths = [
            f_name
            for f_name in datapaths
            if any(f_name.endswith(suffix) for suffix in suffixes)
        ]
    datapaths.sort()  # Sort to ensure deterministic order across ranks
    return datapaths


class OfflineEagle3Dataset(torch.utils.data.Dataset):
    def __init__(
        self,
        datapath,
        transform=None,
        max_len=2048,
        ttt_length=1,
        use_usp_preprocess=False,
    ):
        """
        Args:
            datapath: List of file paths.
            transform: Optional transform to apply.
            max_len: Maximum sequence length to load.
            ttt_length: TTT overlap length used in USP preprocessing.
            use_usp_preprocess: Whether to shard all sequences with USP overlap in preprocessing.
        """
        self.datapaths = datapath
        self.transform = transform
        self._epoch = 0
        self.max_len = max_len
        self.ttt_length = ttt_length
        self.use_usp_preprocess = use_usp_preprocess
        if use_usp_preprocess:
            sp_group = get_draft_sp_group()
            self.sp_rank = torch.distributed.get_rank(sp_group)
            self.sp_size = torch.distributed.get_world_size(sp_group)
            ring_group = get_sp_ring_group()
            self.ring_rank = torch.distributed.get_rank(ring_group)
            self.sp_ring_size = torch.distributed.get_world_size(ring_group)

    @staticmethod
    def process_data(data, max_len, transform=None):
        new_data = {}
        # Squeeze due to our data generation script adding a batch dimension
        hidden_state = data["aux_hidden_state"].squeeze(0)[:max_len][None, :]
        target = data["hidden_state"].squeeze(0)[:max_len][None, :]

        input_ids = data["input_ids"][:max_len][None, :]
        loss_mask = data["loss_mask"][:max_len][None, :]
        loss_mask[0, -1] = 0

        new_data["attention_mask"] = torch.ones_like(loss_mask, dtype=torch.long)
        new_data["loss_mask"] = loss_mask
        new_data["target"] = target
        new_data["hidden_state"] = hidden_state
        new_data["input_ids"] = input_ids
        if transform:
            new_data = transform(new_data)
        return new_data

    @staticmethod
    def process_data_usp(
        data,
        max_len,
        ttt_length=1,
        transform=None,
        sp_rank=0,
        sp_size=1,
        ring_rank=0,
        sp_ring_size=1,
    ):
        """
        USP preprocess: shard all sequences by sp_rank and add TTT overlap.
        Each local sequence length = ceil(max_len / sp_size) + ttt_length.
        """
        new_data = {}

        input_ids = data["input_ids"]
        if input_ids.ndim == 1:
            input_ids = input_ids.unsqueeze(0)

        global_len = min(max_len, input_ids.shape[1])
        chunk_size = (global_len + sp_size - 1) // sp_size
        start = sp_rank * chunk_size
        local_len = chunk_size + ttt_length

        end = min(start + local_len, global_len)

        def _slice_and_pad(tensor):
            if tensor.ndim == 1:
                tensor = tensor.unsqueeze(0)
            tensor = tensor[:, :global_len]
            sliced = tensor[:, start : min(end, tensor.shape[1])]
            valid_len = sliced.shape[1]
            if valid_len < local_len:
                pad_len = local_len - valid_len
                if tensor.ndim == 2:
                    sliced = F.pad(sliced, (0, pad_len))
                else:
                    sliced = F.pad(sliced, (0, 0, 0, pad_len))
            return sliced.contiguous(), valid_len

        if "aux_hidden_state" not in data or data["aux_hidden_state"] is None:
            raise KeyError("aux_hidden_state is required for OfflineEagle3Dataset")
        new_data["hidden_state"], _ = _slice_and_pad(data["aux_hidden_state"])
        new_data["target"], _ = _slice_and_pad(data["hidden_state"])

        new_data["input_ids"], valid_len = _slice_and_pad(input_ids)

        full_loss_mask = data["loss_mask"]
        if full_loss_mask.ndim == 1:
            full_loss_mask = full_loss_mask.unsqueeze(0)

        full_loss_mask = full_loss_mask[:, :global_len].clone()
        if full_loss_mask.numel() > 0:
            full_loss_mask[0, -1] = 0
        new_data["loss_mask"], _ = _slice_and_pad(full_loss_mask)

        local_len = new_data["input_ids"].shape[1]
        attention_mask = torch.zeros((1, local_len), dtype=torch.long)
        attention_mask[:, :valid_len] = 1
        new_data["attention_mask"] = attention_mask

        # Position ids should align with Ulysses all2all-expanded sequence length.
        # Within each ring group there are sp_ulysses_size Ulysses peers; each holds a
        # distinct usp_chunk_size slice, so position IDs must differ by ulysses_rank offset.
        sp_ulysses_size = max(1, sp_size // sp_ring_size)
        usp_chunk_size = max(local_len - ttt_length, 0)
        ring_chunk = usp_chunk_size * sp_ulysses_size
        ulysses_rank = sp_rank % sp_ulysses_size
        ring_start = ring_rank * ring_chunk + ulysses_rank * usp_chunk_size
        new_data["position_ids"] = torch.arange(
            ring_start, ring_start + usp_chunk_size, dtype=torch.long
        ).unsqueeze(0)

        if transform:
            new_data = transform(new_data)

        return new_data

    def __len__(self):
        return len(self.datapaths)

    def _open_file(self, index):
        """
        Opens the file with memory mapping.
        This operation is virtually instant and consumes negligible RAM
        because no data is actually read from disk yet.
        """
        data_path = self.datapaths[index]
        if data_path.endswith(".gz"):
            with gzip.open(data_path, "rb") as f:
                return torch.load(io.BytesIO(f.read()), weights_only=False)
        return torch.load(data_path, weights_only=False, mmap=True)

    def __getitem__(self, index):
        try:
            data = self._open_file(index)
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"ERROR Failed to load {self.datapaths[index]} with error {e}")
            data = self._open_file(0)

        # 2. Read only specific bytes from disk
        if self.use_usp_preprocess:
            return self.process_data_usp(
                data,
                self.max_len,
                ttt_length=self.ttt_length,
                transform=self.transform,
                sp_rank=self.sp_rank,
                sp_size=self.sp_size,
                ring_rank=self.ring_rank,
                sp_ring_size=self.sp_ring_size,
            )
        return self.process_data(
            data,
            self.max_len,
            self.transform,
        )

    def set_epoch(self, epoch):
        self._epoch = epoch


def build_offline_eagle3_dataset(
    hidden_states_path: str,
    max_len: int = 2048,
    ttt_length: int = 1,
    use_usp_preprocess: bool = False,
) -> torch.utils.data.Dataset:

    return OfflineEagle3Dataset(
        list_local_files(hidden_states_path),
        max_len=max_len,
        ttt_length=ttt_length,
        use_usp_preprocess=use_usp_preprocess,
    )


# ==============================
# Vocab Mapping
# ==============================
def generate_vocab_mapping_file(
    dataset: HFDataset,
    target_vocab_size: int,
    draft_vocab_size: int,
    cache_dir: str = "./cache/vocab_mapping",
    cache_key: str = "vocab_mapping",
) -> str:
    """
    Generate a vocab mapping file for the dataset.

    Args:
        dataset: The dataset to process.
        target_vocab_size: The target vocabulary size.
        draft_vocab_size: The draft vocabulary size.
        cache_dir: The directory to use for caching the vocab mapping file.
        cache_key: The key to use for caching the vocab mapping file.

    Returns:
        The path to the vocab mapping file.
    """
    # prepare cache directory
    os.makedirs(cache_dir, exist_ok=True)
    vocab_mapping_path = os.path.join(cache_dir, f"{cache_key}.pt")

    if os.path.exists(vocab_mapping_path):
        print(f"Loading vocab mapping from the cached file at: {vocab_mapping_path}")
        return vocab_mapping_path

    # we first count the frequency of effective tokens in the dataset
    token_dict = Counter()
    for input_ids, loss_mask in tqdm(
        zip(dataset["input_ids"], dataset["loss_mask"]),
        total=len(dataset),
        desc="Counting tokens for vocab mapping",
    ):
        masked_ids = input_ids[loss_mask == 1]
        unique_ids, counts = masked_ids.unique(return_counts=True)
        batch_token_dict = dict(zip(unique_ids.tolist(), counts.tolist()))
        token_dict.update(batch_token_dict)

    # generate the d2t and t2d mapping
    d2t, t2d = process_token_dict_to_mappings(
        token_dict,
        draft_vocab_size,
        target_vocab_size,
    )

    vocab_mapping = {
        "d2t": d2t,
        "t2d": t2d,
    }
    torch.save(vocab_mapping, vocab_mapping_path)
    print(f"Saved vocab mapping to: {vocab_mapping_path}")
    return vocab_mapping_path


def process_offline_eagle3_sample(
    raw: Dict[str, torch.Tensor], max_len: int
) -> Dict[str, torch.Tensor]:
    """Normalize one prepared EAGLE3 feature sample for the canonical loader.

    Hidden-state preparation stores the target final state under
    hidden_state and auxiliary layers under aux_hidden_state. Training consumes
    those tensors as target and hidden_state respectively.
    """
    hidden_state = raw["aux_hidden_state"].squeeze(0)[:max_len].unsqueeze(0)
    target = raw["hidden_state"].squeeze(0)[:max_len].unsqueeze(0)
    input_ids = raw["input_ids"][:max_len].unsqueeze(0)
    loss_mask = raw["loss_mask"][:max_len].clone().unsqueeze(0)
    if loss_mask.numel() > 0:
        loss_mask[0, -1] = 0

    return {
        "attention_mask": torch.ones_like(loss_mask, dtype=torch.long),
        "loss_mask": loss_mask,
        "target": target,
        "hidden_state": hidden_state,
        "input_ids": input_ids,
    }


def process_offline_dflash_sample(
    raw: Dict[str, torch.Tensor], max_len: int
) -> Dict[str, torch.Tensor]:
    """Normalize one prepared DFlash-family feature sample.

    DFlash and Domino consume the same capture contract: token ids, a loss
    mask, and the concatenated target-layer states.  Unlike EAGLE3, there is no
    auxiliary/final-state swap and no target distribution.  Offline feature
    files may store ``hidden_states`` as either ``[seq, width]`` or
    ``[1, seq, width]``; the canonical loader always receives a leading batch
    dimension.
    """
    input_ids = raw["input_ids"][:max_len].unsqueeze(0)
    loss_mask = raw["loss_mask"][:max_len].unsqueeze(0)
    hidden_states = raw["hidden_states"]
    if hidden_states.dim() == 3:
        if hidden_states.shape[0] != 1:
            raise ValueError(
                "offline DFlash hidden_states must have shape [seq, width] or "
                f"[1, seq, width], got {tuple(hidden_states.shape)}"
            )
        hidden_states = hidden_states.squeeze(0)
    if hidden_states.dim() != 2:
        raise ValueError(
            "offline DFlash hidden_states must have shape [seq, width] or "
            f"[1, seq, width], got {tuple(hidden_states.shape)}"
        )
    hidden_states = hidden_states[:max_len].unsqueeze(0)

    sequence_lengths = {
        input_ids.shape[1],
        loss_mask.shape[1],
        hidden_states.shape[1],
    }
    if len(sequence_lengths) != 1:
        raise ValueError(
            "offline DFlash features have mismatched sequence lengths after "
            f"truncation: input_ids={input_ids.shape[1]}, "
            f"loss_mask={loss_mask.shape[1]}, "
            f"hidden_states={hidden_states.shape[1]}"
        )
    return {
        "input_ids": input_ids,
        "loss_mask": loss_mask,
        "hidden_states": hidden_states,
    }


def process_token_dict_to_mappings(
    token_dict: Counter,
    draft_vocab_size: int,
    target_vocab_size: int,
) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
    """
    Process token_dict to create d2t and t2d mappings, with optional caching.

    Args:
        token_dict: A Counter object mapping token ids to their frequencies.
        draft_vocab_size: The size of the draft vocabulary.
        target_vocab_size: The size of the target vocabulary.

    Returns:
        A tuple containing:
            - d2t: A tensor mapping draft token ids to target token ids.
            - t2d: A tensor mapping target token ids to draft token ids.
    """
    if len(token_dict) < draft_vocab_size:
        existing_tokens = set(token_dict.keys())
        missing_tokens = set(range(draft_vocab_size)) - existing_tokens
        for token in missing_tokens:
            token_dict[token] = 0
            if len(token_dict) >= draft_vocab_size:
                break
    print(f"Added missing tokens to reach draft vocab size: {draft_vocab_size}")
    print(f"Total tokens after addition: {len(token_dict)}")
    total_frequency = sum(token_dict.values())
    top_N = token_dict.most_common(draft_vocab_size)
    top_N_frequency_sum = sum(freq for key, freq in top_N)

    if total_frequency == 0:
        print(
            "Warning: Total token frequency is zero. All tokens will have zero ratio."
        )
        top_N_ratio = 0.0
    else:
        top_N_ratio = top_N_frequency_sum / total_frequency

    print(f"top {draft_vocab_size} token frequency ratio: {top_N_ratio:.2%}")
    used_tokens = [key for key, freq in top_N]
    used_tokens.sort()

    d2t = [used_tokens[i] - i for i in range(len(used_tokens))]
    t2d = [i in used_tokens for i in range(target_vocab_size)]
    d2t = torch.tensor(d2t)
    t2d = torch.tensor(t2d)

    return d2t, t2d

1	import json
2	import re
3	import warnings
4	from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
5	from typing import Dict, List, Tuple
6	
7	import torch
8	from transformers import PreTrainedTokenizer
9	
10	from .template import ChatTemplate
11	
12	__all__ = ["GeneralParser", "GLMParser", "HarmonyParser", "ThinkingParser"]
13	
14	
15	class Parser(ABC):
16	
17	    def __init__(
18	        self,
19	        tokenizer: PreTrainedTokenizer,
20	        chat_template: ChatTemplate,
21	    ):
22	        self.tokenizer = tokenizer
23	        self.chat_template = chat_template
24	        self.standard_keys = {"role", "content", "tool_calls"}
25	
26	    @abstractmethod
27	    def parse(
28	        self, conversation: "Conversation", max_length: int
29	    ) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
30	        """
31	        Parse the conversation into a list of tensors.
32	
33	        Args:
34	            conversation: The conversation to parse.
35	
36	        Returns:
37	            A list of tensors: [input_ids, loss_mask]
38	        """
39	
40	    def _sanitize_message(self, message: dict) -> dict:
41	        """
42	        Clean up individual messages, handling the following issues:
43	        1. `tool_calls` is a string → Parse as a list
44	        2. `tool_calls[].function.arguments` is a string → Parse as a dictionary
45	        3. Non-standard fields (extra, etc.) in `tool_calls[]` → Remove
46	        """
47	        cleaned = {k: v for k, v in message.items() if k in self.standard_keys}
48	
49	        # ===== handle tool_calls =====
50	        if "tool_calls" in cleaned:
51	            tool_calls = cleaned["tool_calls"]
52	
53	            # tool_calls is a string → Parsing
54	            if isinstance(tool_calls, str):
55	                try:
56	                    tool_calls = json.loads(tool_calls)
57	                except json.JSONDecodeError:
58	                    warnings.warn(
59	                        f"Failed to parse tool_calls JSON string, removing tool_calls"
60	                    )
61	                    cleaned.pop("tool_calls", None)
62	                    return cleaned
63	
64	            # Clean each tool_call
65	            if isinstance(tool_calls, list):
66	                sanitized_tool_calls = []
67	
68	                for tc in tool_calls:
69	                    if not isinstance(tc, dict):
70	                        continue
71	
72	                    # Only retain the standard fields: id, type, function
73	                    clean_tc = {
74	                        "id": tc.get("id", ""),
75	                        "type": tc.get("type", "function"),
76	                    }
77	
78	                    # handle function
79	                    func = tc.get("function", {})
80	                    if isinstance(func, dict):
81	                        clean_func = {
82	                            "name": func.get("name", ""),
83	                        }
84	
85	                        arguments = func.get("arguments", {})
86	                        if isinstance(arguments, str):
87	                            try:
88	                                arguments = json.loads(arguments)
89	                            except json.JSONDecodeError:
90	                                warnings.warn(
91	                                    f"Failed to parse arguments for tool '{clean_func['name']}': "
92	                                    f"{arguments[:100]}..."
93	                                )
94	                                arguments = {}
95	
96	                        clean_func["arguments"] = arguments
97	                        clean_tc["function"] = clean_func
98	
99	                    sanitized_tool_calls.append(clean_tc)
100	
101	                cleaned["tool_calls"] = sanitized_tool_calls
102	
103	        return cleaned
104	
105	    def _normalize_message(self, message: dict) -> dict:
106	        role = message.get("role", message.get("from", ""))
107	        content = message.get("content") or message.get("value") or ""
108	
109	        if role in ("human", "user"):
110	            role = "user"
111	        elif role in ("gpt", "assistant"):
112	            role = "assistant"
113	
114	        normalized = {**message, "role": role, "content": content}
115	        normalized.pop("from", None)
116	        normalized.pop("value", None)
117	        return normalized
118	
119	
120	_harmony_encoding = None
121	
122	
123	class GeneralParser(Parser):
124	
125	    def __init__(
126	        self,
127	        tokenizer: PreTrainedTokenizer,
128	        chat_template: ChatTemplate,
129	    ):
130	        super().__init__(tokenizer, chat_template)
131	        self.system_prompt = chat_template.system_prompt
132	        self.user_message_separator = f"{chat_template.end_of_turn_token}"
133	        self.assistant_message_separator = f"{chat_template.assistant_header}"
134	        self.set_assistant_pattern(chat_template)
135	
136	    def apply_chat_template(self, messages, tool, **kwargs) -> str:
137	        conversation = self.tokenizer.apply_chat_template(
138	            messages,
139	            tokenize=False,
140	            add_generation_prompt=False,
141	            tools=tool if tool else None,
142	            **kwargs,
143	        )
144	        return conversation
145	
146	    def set_assistant_pattern(self, chat_template: ChatTemplate):
147	        if chat_template.assistant_pattern_type == "longcat":
148	            self.assistant_pattern = (
149	                re.escape(self.assistant_message_separator)
150	                + r"([\s\S]*?(?:"
151	                + re.escape("[Round ")
152	                + r"\d+"
153	                + re.escape("] USER:")
154	                + "|$))"
155	            )
156	        elif chat_template.assistant_pattern_type == "inkling":
157	            terminators = "|".join(
158	                re.escape(token)
159	                for token in (
160	                    "<|message_user|>",
161	                    "<|message_tool|>",
162	                    "<|message_system|>",
163	                )
164	            )
165	            self.assistant_pattern = (
166	                re.escape(self.assistant_message_separator)
167	                + r"([\s\S]*?(?:"
168	                + terminators
169	                + "|$))"
170	            )
171	        elif chat_template.assistant_pattern_type == "glm":
172	            self.assistant_pattern = (
173	                re.escape(self.assistant_message_separator)
174	                + r"(?:</think>)?"
175	                + r"([\s\S]*?(?:"
176	                + re.escape(self.chat_template.end_of_turn_token)
177	                + "|$))"
178	            )
179	        else:
180	            self.assistant_pattern = (
181	                re.escape(self.assistant_message_separator)
182	                + r"([\s\S]*?(?:"
183	                + re.escape(self.chat_template.end_of_turn_token)
184	                + "|$))"
185	            )
186	
187	    def parse(
188	        self,
189	        conversation: "Conversation",
190	        max_length: int,
191	        preformatted: bool = False,
192	        train_only_last_turn: bool = False,
193	        tool: List[Dict] = [],
194	        **kwargs,
195	    ) -> Dict[str, List[torch.Tensor]]:
196	        if not preformatted:
197	            conversation = [
198	                self._normalize_message(message) for message in conversation
199	            ]
200	            messages = []
201	
202	            if conversation[0]["role"] == "system":
203	                warnings.warn(
204	                    f"The first message is from system, we will use the system prompt from the data and ignore the system prompt from the template"
205	                )
206	                messages.append(
207	                    {"role": "system", "content": conversation[0]["content"]}
208	                )
209	                conversation = conversation[1:]
210	            else:
211	                if self.system_prompt:
212	                    messages.append({"role": "system", "content": self.system_prompt})
213	
214	            while conversation and conversation[0]["role"] != "user":
215	                warnings.warn(
216	                    f"Dropping leading '{conversation[0]['role']}' message before the first user turn."
217	                )
218	                conversation = conversation[1:]
219	
220	            for j, sentence in enumerate(conversation):
221	                role = sentence["role"]
222	                if j == 0:
223	                    if role != "user":
224	                        warnings.warn(
225	                            f"Conversation must start with a 'user' role, but found '{role}'. Conversation truncated."
226	                        )
227	                        break
228	                else:
229	                    prev_role = conversation[j - 1]["role"]
230	                    if role == "tool" and prev_role not in ["assistant", "tool"]:
231	                        warnings.warn(
232	                            f"A 'tool' message must follow an 'assistant' or 'tool' message, but was preceded by '{prev_role}'. Conversation truncated."
233	                        )
234	                        break
235	                    if role == "assistant" and prev_role not in ["user", "tool"]:
236	                        warnings.warn(
237	                            f"An 'assistant' message must follow a 'user' or 'tool' message, but was preceded by '{prev_role}'. Conversation truncated."
238	                        )
239	                        break
240	                sentence = self._sanitize_message(sentence)
241	                messages.append(sentence)
242	            try:
243	                conversation = self.apply_chat_template(messages, tool=tool, **kwargs)
244	            except (ValueError, TypeError):
245	                # Fallback rendering for tokenizers without built-in chat_template
246	                warnings.warn(
247	                    "Tokenizer does not have a chat_template, using fallback rendering."
248	                )
249	                parts = []
250	                bos_token = getattr(self.tokenizer, "bos_token", None)
251	                user_header = self.chat_template.user_header or ""
252	                assistant_header = self.chat_template.assistant_header or ""
253	                end_of_turn = self.chat_template.end_of_turn_token or ""
254	
255	                # Add BOS token at the start
256	                if bos_token:
257	                    parts.append(bos_token)
258	
259	                for msg in messages:
260	                    if msg["role"] == "system":
261	                        parts.append(msg["content"])
262	                    elif msg["role"] == "user":
263	                        parts.append(f"{user_header}{msg['content']}")
264	                    elif msg["role"] == "assistant":
265	                        parts.append(f"{assistant_header}{msg['content']}{end_of_turn}")
266	                conversation = "".join(parts)
267	
268	        if self.tokenizer.pad_token_id is None:
269	            self.tokenizer.pad_token_id = self.tokenizer.unk_token_id
270	
271	        # get input_ids
272	        encoding = self.tokenizer(
273	            conversation,
274	            max_length=max_length,
275	            truncation=True,
276	            return_tensors="pt",
277	            add_special_tokens=False,
278	        )
279	        input_ids = encoding.input_ids[0]
280	        loss_mask = torch.zeros(len(input_ids), dtype=torch.long)
281	
282	        matches = list(re.finditer(self.assistant_pattern, conversation, re.DOTALL))
283	        if train_only_last_turn and matches:
284	            matches = [matches[-1]]  # Only keep the last match
285	
286	        for match in matches:
287	            content_start_char = match.start(1)
288	            content_end_char = match.end(1)
289	
290	            # --- Core Alternative Operation: Calculate Token Index Based on Prefix String Length ---
291	            # Encode the text "assistant start", the length of which is the position of the starting token.
292	            prefix_ids = self.tokenizer.encode(
293	                conversation[:content_start_char],
294	                add_special_tokens=False,
295	                truncation=True,
296	                max_length=max_length,
297	            )
298	            # Encodes the text "assistant end", the length of which is the position of the end token.
299	            full_ids = self.tokenizer.encode(
300	                conversation[:content_end_char],
301	                add_special_tokens=False,
302	                truncation=True,
303	                max_length=max_length,
304	            )
305	
306	            start_token_idx = len(prefix_ids)
307	            end_token_idx = len(full_ids)
308	
309	            # Handling out-of-bounds errors caused by truncation
310	            actual_start = min(start_token_idx, len(input_ids))
311	            actual_end = min(end_token_idx, len(input_ids))
312	
313	            if actual_start < actual_end:
314	                loss_mask[actual_start:actual_end] = 1
315	
316	        # Zero out loss_mask for ignore_tokens
317	        ignore_tokens = self.chat_template.ignore_token
318	        if ignore_tokens:
319	            for token_str in ignore_tokens:
320	                start = 0
321	                while True:
322	                    idx = conversation.find(token_str, start)
323	                    if idx == -1:
324	                        break
325	                    ignore_start_char = idx
326	                    ignore_end_char = idx + len(token_str)
327	
328	                    prefix_ids = self.tokenizer.encode(
329	                        conversation[:ignore_start_char],
330	                        add_special_tokens=False,
331	                        truncation=True,
332	                        max_length=max_length,
333	                    )
334	                    full_ids = self.tokenizer.encode(
335	                        conversation[:ignore_end_char],
336	                        add_special_tokens=False,
337	                        truncation=True,
338	                        max_length=max_length,
339	                    )
340	
341	                    start_token_idx = min(len(prefix_ids), len(input_ids))
342	                    end_token_idx = min(len(full_ids), len(input_ids))
343	
344	                    if start_token_idx < end_token_idx:
345	                        loss_mask[start_token_idx:end_token_idx] = 0
346	
347	                    start = ignore_end_char
348	
349	        return input_ids, loss_mask
350	
351	
352	class HarmonyParser(Parser):
353	    def __init__(self, tokenizer: PreTrainedTokenizer, chat_template: ChatTemplate):
354	        super().__init__(tokenizer, chat_template)
355	        self.reasoning_levels = ["low", "medium", "high"]
356	        self.default_reasoning_level = "low"
357	
358	    def build_single_turn_prompt(
359	        self,
360	        prompt_text: str,
361	        role: str,
362	        content: str,
363	    ) -> str:
364	        """Embed user message into the required prompt template."""
365	        if role == "system":
366	            prompt_text = f"<|start|>system<|message|>{content}<|end|>"
367	        elif role == "assistant_reasoning_effort":
368	            prompt_text = f"<|start|>system<|message|>You are ChatGPT, a large language model trained by OpenAI.\nKnowledge cutoff: 2024-06\nCurrent date: 2025-06-28\n\nReasoning: {content.lower()}\n\n# Valid channels: analysis, commentary, final. Channel must be included for every message.<|end|>"
369	        elif role == "user":
370	            prompt_text += f"<|start|>user<|message|>{content}<|end|>"
371	        elif role == "assistant_analysis":
372	            prompt_text += (
373	                f"<|start|>assistant<|channel|>analysis<|message|>{content}<|end|>"
374	            )
375	        elif role == "assistant_commentary":
376	            prompt_text += (
377	                f"<|start|>assistant<|channel|>commentary<|message|>{content}<|end|>"
378	            )
379	        elif role == "assistant_final":
380	            prompt_text += (
381	                f"<|start|>assistant<|channel|>final<|message|>{content}<|end|>"
382	            )
383	        else:
384	            raise ValueError(f"Unknown role: {role}")
385	        return prompt_text
386	
387	    def parse(
388	        self,
389	        conversation: "Conversation",
390	        max_length: int,
391	        preformatted: bool = False,
392	        train_only_last_turn: bool = False,
393	        tool: List[Dict] = [],
394	    ) -> List[torch.Tensor]:
395	        if not preformatted:
396	            prompt_text = ""
397	            for j, message in enumerate(conversation):
398	                if j == 0 and (
399	                    message["role"] != "system"
400	                    and message["role"] != "assistant_reasoning_effort"
401	                ):
402	                    prompt_text = self.build_single_turn_prompt(
403	                        prompt_text,
404	                        "assistant_reasoning_effort",
405	                        self.default_reasoning_level,
406	                    )
407	                prompt_text = self.build_single_turn_prompt(
408	                    prompt_text, message["role"], message["content"]
409	                )
410	            conversation = prompt_text
411	
412	        if self.tokenizer.pad_token_id is None:
413	            self.tokenizer.pad_token_id = self.tokenizer.unk_token_id
414	
415	        encoding = self.tokenizer(
416	            conversation,
417	            return_offsets_mapping=True,
418	            max_length=max_length,
419	            truncation=True,
420	            return_tensors="pt",
421	            add_special_tokens=False,
422	        )
423	        input_ids = encoding.input_ids[0]
424	        offsets = encoding.offset_mapping[0]
425	        loss_mask = torch.zeros(len(input_ids), dtype=torch.long)
426	
427	        # Find spans of assistant responses using regex
428	        # We match `<|start|>assistant` and only extract the content following it.
429	        # This continues until `<|start|>user<|message|>` appears, or until the end of the string.
430	        pattern = re.compile(
431	            r"<\|start\|>assistant([\s\S]*?)(?=<\|start\|>user<\|message\|>|$)"
432	        )
433	
434	        # Find all matching segments
435	        matches = list(pattern.finditer(conversation))
436	        if train_only_last_turn and matches:
437	            matches = [matches[-1]]  # Only keep the last match
438	
439	        for match in matches:
440	            # match.start(0) is the start index of the full match (including `<|start|>assistant`)
441	            # match.start(1) is the start index of the first capture group (excluding `<|start|>assistant`)
442	            # match.end(1) is the end index of the content
443	            start_char = match.start(1)
444	            end_char = match.end(1)
445	
446	            # Map character indices to token indices
447	            for idx, (ts, te) in enumerate(offsets):
448	                # Set mask to 1 only if the token's character range falls entirely within the "content area"
449	                if ts >= start_char and te <= end_char:
450	                    loss_mask[idx] = 1
451	
452	        return input_ids, loss_mask
453	
454	
455	class ThinkingParser(GeneralParser):
456	    """
457	    Parser for thinking/reasoning models.
458	
459	    This parser processes the entire conversation (not just the last turn).
460	    It handles reasoning_content and tool_calls in assistant messages.
461	    The loss mask covers from assistant_header to end_of_turn_token (inclusive).
462	    """
463	
464	    def __init__(
465	        self,
466	        tokenizer: PreTrainedTokenizer,
467	        chat_template: ChatTemplate,
468	    ):
469	        super().__init__(tokenizer, chat_template)
470	        self.standard_keys = {
471	            "role",
472	            "content",
473	            "tool_calls",
474	            "reasoning_content",
475	            "name",
476	            "tool_call_id",
477	        }
478	
479	    def apply_chat_template(self, messages, tool, **kwargs) -> str:
480	        """Apply chat template to all messages, handling reasoning_content and tool_calls."""
481	        # See GeneralParser.apply_chat_template: pass `None` rather than an empty
482	        # list so templates don't enter tool-use mode when there are no tools.
483	        conversation = self.tokenizer.apply_chat_template(
484	            messages,
485	            tokenize=False,
486	            add_generation_prompt=False,
487	            add_special_tokens=False,
488	            tools=tool if tool else None,
489	            **kwargs,
490	        )
491	        return conversation
492	
493	    def parse(
494	        self,
495	        conversation: "Conversation",
496	        max_length: int,
497	        preformatted: bool = False,
498	        train_only_last_turn: bool = False,
499	        tool: List[Dict] = [],
500	        **kwargs,
501	    ) -> Dict[str, List[torch.Tensor]]:
502	        """Parse conversation, processing all assistant turns for loss mask."""
503	        if self.chat_template.enable_thinking:
504	            kwargs["enable_thinking"] = True
505	        return super().parse(
506	            conversation, max_length, preformatted, train_only_last_turn, tool, **kwargs
507	        )
508	
509	
510	class GLMParser(GeneralParser):
511	    """Render GLM-5.2's hybrid-thinking template consistently for training."""
512	
513	    def apply_chat_template(self, messages, tool, **kwargs) -> str:
514	        kwargs.setdefault("enable_thinking", False)
515	        return super().apply_chat_template(messages, tool, **kwargs)
516
